note: Advisory sandbox build. The deliverable is a MOCK-data lead-intelligence dashboard; its proof is two red-capable teeth: (A) the rendered priority_tag breakdown must equal an independent Counter over the mock rows, and (B) the repo language_gate dogfood over the rendered HTML (CHECK_OK clean, CHECK_REJECTED when an overclaim is injected). Never a bare governance PASS.
title: Gateway Lead-Intelligence Dashboard (MOCK) — priority_tag breakdown renderer + template

subject.note: renders a mock priority_tag breakdown dashboard grounded in the gateway blueprint §4 (deterministic priority rule), §5 (gateway_leads schema) and §7 (v1 scope vs DEFERRED); judged by the repo language_gate (scan/decide, surface=public). Sources read-only; all writes confined to catalog/builds/CO-6.

what_it_proves: the dashboard renders the priority_tag breakdown (high / medium / low) over MOCK gateway_leads rows synthesised per blueprint §5 schema; counts observed high=5 medium=4 low=3 over 13 mock rows
MOCK-ONLY: every row is a fabricated placeholder (contacts are *.invalid), no real lead / no PII; test_no_pii_only_placeholder_contacts enforces it

EMPTY-SINK guard: a row with null priority_tag lands in an explicit UNPOPULATED bucket (count=1), excluded from the populated-share denominator — never silently folded into 'low'/0; null contact fields render an UNPOPULATED badge

Lock 10: the generated HTML is written only to a local out/ path and carries a visible 'DO NOT PUSH — not for public hosting' banner plus a MOCK-DATA watermark; every computed metric is watermarked 'SYNTHETIC — not a guaranteed claim'
DEFERRED items (learned/weighted scoring, autonomous routing) render LOCKED / proof-gated, never 'available'; the dashboard makes no autonomy or guaranteed-outcome claim

TOOTH A (data-driven) — the rendered breakdown equals an INDEPENDENT Counter over the mock rows; mutating one row's priority_tag (high->low) moves the independent count and breaks the assertion, so it discriminates real vs mutated input
TOOTH B (dogfood) — the REAL rendered dashboard does NOT FAIL the language_gate (public surface) -> CHECK_OK (decision=WARN on undefined title-case terms only, no OVERCLAIM/BANNED_REGISTER)

NEGATIVE — a MINIMAL single-field mutation of that same on-disk artifact ('not a guaranteed claim' -> '100% guaranteed certified') makes the gate FAIL on OVERCLAIM -> CHECK_REJECTED; reversing the injection reproduces the clean artifact byte-for-byte (gate never relaxed)
the dogfood uses scan/decide directly, writing NO receipt into the tracked language_gate/receipts dir
locks_respected: Lock 5 append-only (sources + language_gate read-only; all writes confined to catalog/builds/CO-6; no rm/edit/overwrite of any tracked file)
Lock 10 commercial (generated HTML to local out/ only, visible DO-NOT-PUSH banner + MOCK-DATA watermark, DEFERRED tiers LOCKED, no autonomy/outcome claim, no CF/Railway/Vercel path)
no live call (no network/CF/Supabase/sends/deploy; gate run in-process with no receipt write; HOME=temp)
worktree not pushed; no git write commands

results.breakdown: high=5 medium=4 low=3 unpopulated=1 (of 13 mock rows)
results.red_run_canary: FIRED (deliberately-wrong assertion high==99 vs real high=5 raised AssertionError)
results.pollution_outside_build_dir: none (git status --porcelain tracked changes outside CO-6 == 0)

exit_gate_B4: CO-6 done — mock gateway lead-intel dashboard renders the priority_tag breakdown, counts match the mock rows (data-driven tooth), dogfoods clean through the language_gate, and rejects an injected overclaim by minimal mutation.
next_action: wire the same renderer to a live-but-read-only gateway_leads view once real rows exist (RLS read policy); keep learned scoring LOCKED until data supports thresholds (§4, §7).
